description: Exceptions in launch propagate through the coroutine scope, while async stores failures until await.
body: Use when choosing coroutine builders. Do not replace await with launch if caller needs result/error semantics. Keep structured result ownership clear and test failure paths. Verify negative coroutine tests. Risk boundary is hidden failed work or premature success. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
